finding: PermissionCheck + PermissionList (+ tuple impls for 1/2/3/4) + PermissionInfo diffed line-for-line against the pre-extraction permissions/types.rs: byte-identical, including resource()/action() split defaults, to_info() projection, and format_description single-vs-ALL branching. Only the file location changed.

types.rs is now `pub use ziee_identity::{PermissionCheck, PermissionInfo, PermissionList};`. mod.rs's `pub use types::{...}`, openapi.rs's `use super::types::PermissionList`, and user/permissions.rs's `use crate::modules::permissions::{PermissionCheck, PermissionInfo}` all resolve unchanged; the public path `crate::modules::permissions::PermissionCheck` is preserved.

PermissionInfo has 0 occurrences in the committed baseline openapi.ui.json + types.ui.ts, so relocating it (JsonSchema derive intact) registers no new schema and cannot alter the client contract. Verified types.ts regenerates BYTE-IDENTICAL to baseline; openapi.json canonically-equal.

check_permissions_array body byte-identical to the pre-extraction private fn in checker.rs: exact-match contains -> full `*` contains -> hierarchical prefix loop (for i in 1..parts.len() { format!("{}::*", prefix) }). Only `fn`->`pub fn` (T-1). Wildcard/hierarchical semantics unchanged.

No over-grant introduced: empty-set denies, a lone `*` short-circuits, a deeper wildcard does NOT match a shallower required perm, and a sibling namespace is not covered. Ported unit tests (exact/full-wildcard/resource-wildcard/hierarchical-depth/empty-deny) pass in ziee-identity; the 14 concrete checker.rs tests still pass in ziee against the same body via check_permission_union.

check_permission_union retained verbatim in ziee: user-direct check, then per active group (is_active guard) check, both delegating to the moved check_permissions_array with identical argument order. Delegation is call-for-call unchanged; ~20 call sites across the codebase are untouched.

`use ziee_identity::check_permissions_array;` is used by check_permission_union (2 call sites), so it is not an unused import under the workspace unused_imports=deny lint. cargo check -p ziee is warning-clean for this module (the only 4 build warnings are pre-existing dead_code in scheduler/models.rs + mcp/repository.rs, unrelated to B1b).

impl Principal for User exposes is_admin()->self.is_admin and direct_permissions()->&self.permissions; active_group_permissions() uses the default (empty). has_permission is therefore a direct-perms-only UNION today and, critically, does NOT fold in is_admin (matching check_permission_union). Since no ziee call site calls User::has_permission yet (extractor stays until B3), the live authorization path is unchanged — the impl is additive/inert.

Principal is the minimal enforcement interface: is_admin + direct_permissions + active_group_permissions + provided has_permission reusing check_permissions_array. Default active_group_permissions() returns empty so a data-only identity (User) needs just two methods; the group dimension is opt-in and wired in B3. No concrete type referenced.

impl TokenVerifier for JwtService delegates verify_access_token/verify_refresh_token to the existing validate_access_token/validate_refresh_token (unchanged: issuer/audience set, JWT_LEEWAY_SECONDS, `-refresh` audience for refresh, AppError mapping). No validation logic altered; the interface carries ziee's concrete Claims/AppError via associated types so ziee-identity stays free of jsonwebtoken/AppError.

TokenVerifier has associated Claims + Error types (no jsonwebtoken/AppError dep leaks into ziee-identity). Separate access/refresh methods mirror ziee's two audiences. Concrete JwtService stays in ziee and implements it — pluggable token scheme (decision #1).

All permission-trait unit tests (format_description single/multi, 3-/4-tuple permission lists, resource/action split, to_info projection + JSON shape) moved verbatim into ziee-identity and pass (22 tests total across permission/rbac/principal green in `cargo test -p ziee-identity`).

serde 1.0.219 + schemars 0.9.0 (chrono04,uuid1) pinned to ziee's workspace catalog so src-app/Cargo.lock unifies without a duplicate build; serde_json is a dev-dep for the to_info JSON-shape test. ziee-core dep retained per plan (build-DB-free). `cd sdk && cargo check --workspace` exit 0.

Crate root re-exports PermissionCheck/PermissionInfo/PermissionList/Principal/check_permissions_array/TokenVerifier. No `ziee-chat` / phibya references. Module names (permission/rbac/principal/token) match the abstraction taxonomy.
